title: Alien: Romulus Bursts Into Theaters With $108 Million Global Opening Weekend
text: The science fiction horror sequel "Alien: Romulus" exceeded box office expectations with a strong worldwide debut. Director Fede Álvarez's entry in the long-running franchise earned $45.1 million domestically and $66.7 million internationally for a combined $108.2 million opening weekend. This performance marks the second-best domestic opening in franchise history, behind only "Prometheus" which launched with $51 million in 2012. The film benefited from a relatively modest budget of approximately $80 million before marketing costs, with production taking place in Hungary to utilize local tax incentives. Audience reception has been largely positive. The film secured a B+ CinemaScore—the franchise's highest rating aside from James Cameron's "Aliens" (1986), which received an A. Critics gave it an 81% rating on Rotten Tomatoes. Viewers particularly praised the practical effects-heavy production and creature design, along with performances from David Jonsson as the synthetic character Andy and Cailee Spaeny as protagonist Rain. However, fan reactions proved mixed regarding the film's heavy fan service elements, with some criticizing repetitive callbacks to earlier films.
